keyPoints: Bootstrap: Resampling with replacement to estimate variability
Confidence Interval: 95% CI is commonly used
Normal Distribution: Bell-shaped, symmetric, and continuous
Long Tailed Distribution: Skewed, asymmetric, and has extreme values
Student's-t Distribution: Used for small samples with unknown variance
Binomial Distribution: Discrete, counts outcomes of independent trials
Chi-Square Distribution: Used for goodness-of-fit tests and independence
F Distribution: Used for comparing variances of two groups
Poisson Distribution: Models count data with fixed intervals
Related Distributions: Exponential, Gamma, and Inverse Gamma
